title: C4ISR Software Integration
entry_mechanism: Saudi software companies with defence clearance pathway through PSAP Track 1
executive_summary:
Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C4ISR) systems are the nervous system of modern military operations. Saudi Arabia fields a sophisticated C4ISR architecture — including Link 16 tactical data links, AFATDS artillery fire control, and various intelligence fusion systems — almost all operated and maintained by US and European contractors. Node DE-1 targets the establishment of Saudi software engineering capability to build integration layers, Arabic-language interfaces, and data management modules for non-ITAR C4ISR systems.

The opportunity is clearly defined: C4ISR integration software that runs on top of OEM systems — middleware, data fusion layers, Arabic-language operator interfaces, and Saudi-specific tactical picture management — does not require ITAR-controlled technology and can be developed by Saudi engineers with appropriate security clearances. This is the defence software equivalent of Node MA-4 (UAV software) — the same logic, applied to ground-based C4ISR.

The global C4ISR market was valued at $125 billion in 2024, growing at 4.8% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's annual C4ISR services spend is estimated at $600–900 million — overwhelmingly foreign-contracted today. A Saudi C4ISR software capability that captures even 10% of this spend within Phase I would represent $60–90 million in annual Saudi-retained revenue.

companies.name: Systematic
companies.rationale: Systematic's SitaWare C2 platform is used by NATO forces and is available without ITAR restrictions. Systematic has Middle East defence engagement and would support Saudi integration on top of their platform.
companies.contact: Systematic VP International Defence, Middle East

companies.name: Rohde & Schwarz
companies.rationale: R&S supplies communications systems to the Saudi military and has SDK/API frameworks for integration. Non-ITAR German technology with established Saudi presence.
companies.contact: Rohde & Schwarz VP Government and Defence, Middle East

companies.name: Thales
companies.rationale: Thales has C4ISR products across multiple domains (land, naval, air) with Saudi Arabia relationships. Non-ITAR product lines support Saudi integration work.

companies.name: BAE Systems Applied Intelligence
companies.rationale: BAE Applied Intelligence builds intelligence data management and analytics systems for defence. Active Saudi Arabia engagement through other BAE programmes.
companies.contact: BAE Applied Intelligence VP Middle East

companies.name: Elbit Systems C4I
companies.rationale: Elbit is a world leader in C4ISR integration. Subject to Saudi-Israeli normalisation progress.

partners.name: STC Solutions
partners.current: Large-scale government IT integration; secure communications; data centre operations
partners.upgrade: Establish defence C4ISR software division; NSCS security clearance; Systematic SitaWare integration partnership; Arabic C2 interface development
partners.timeline: 18 months to first C4ISR integration delivery

partners.name: Elm Company
partners.current: Government platform development; secure data management; Arabic NLP
partners.upgrade: Intelligence data management platform for defence; NSCS clearance; C4ISR data fusion layer development

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Digital / SRDP
partners.current: Large-scale data platform operations; real-time monitoring systems; geospatial data
partners.upgrade: Dual-use C4ISR data platform; Saudi geospatial intelligence layer development; real-time sensor fusion for defence applications

partners.name: KACST AI Research Centre
partners.current: AI, computer vision, signal processing research
partners.upgrade: Defence AI programme for ISR data analysis; Saudi-specific threat detection algorithms; GADD-funded intelligence analytics research
